description: Live A↔B bridge mesh measurements from the v0.5.x federation bring-up.

provenance.source: live bridge mesh — two-node federation (ciris-canonical-trio-1 + ciris-status-1)
notes: These are attested measurements from the live two-node mesh, NOT reproducible on a CI runner (no two-node mesh in CI).
They are presented as ATTESTED provenance — each number has a source, date, and node identity. CI criterion numbers are separate (MEASURED on-runner).

The cohort propagation numbers (N=50/100/200) come from the mesh_propagation example (examples/mesh_propagation/main.rs) running in-process on the same hardware; the group-B isolation=0 is a structural property of the cohort gate (the publisher's cohort closure NEVER includes group-B peer ids, so no claim can reach them by construction).

capacity_scoring.algorithm: n_eff_pr (participation ratio of the lens constraint eigenspectrum)
capacity_scoring.description: Node A derives per-agent n_eff from ingested traces → n_eff_pr → capacity(n_eff_pr, gate=20, target=8) → [0,1] score → emits capacity:sustained_coherence:v1 attestation (hybrid-signed CEG scores row).

live_observations.replication_protocol: consent:replication:v1, mutual key-reg, each node owns its corpus
live_observations.note: The live capacity score value depends on the corpus (agent traces ingested). With a thin corpus (<20 traces) the gate returns 0.0 honestly. With a corpus meeting the gate the score is in [0,1]. The gate semantics are: emit 0.0 rather than skip — a zero-row is 'observed but not yet scored', not 'missing'.

cohort_propagation.runtime: ciris_edge::swarm::FountainSwarmRuntime (same runtime src/holonomic.rs wires into production)
cohort_propagation.transport_in_measurement: in-memory bus (measures swarm LOGIC + cohort gate; not wire/Reticulum — see examples/mesh_propagation/main.rs)
cohort_propagation.publisher: node 0 in group A; cohort closure = group-A peers only
cohort_propagation.group_b_isolation_guarantee: Structural — the publisher's cohort closure is derived from replication_peers_from_consent(...), which returns ONLY consented peers. A peer not in the consent graph is NEVER in the cohort closure and cannot receive a FountainHoldingClaim from this publisher regardless of network position. Zero leakage is not a statistical result — it is enforced by construction.

replication_ab.corpus_ownership: each node owns its own corpus (A does not own B's objects, and vice versa)
replication_ab.note: The live A↔B replication latency (network RTT) is not measured here — it is dominated by the Reticulum transport hop, which is bandwidth/RTT-bound, not CPU-bound. The CPU cost per replicated trace is measured by the criterion bench.
